title: Getting Started with TRACLUS
text: TRACLUS package implements trajectory clustering algorithm Lee, Han & Whang (SIGMOD 2007). algorithm works three phases: Partition trajectories line segments using Minimum Description Length (MDL). Cluster segments via DBSCAN density-based spatial clustering. Represent cluster representative trajectory using sweep-line algorithm. vignette walks complete workflow using built-toy dataset.

title: Getting Started with TRACLUS
code: library(TRACLUS)

title: Getting Started with TRACLUS
text: TRACLUS expects data.frame columns trajectory ID, x-coordinate, y-coordinate. built-traclus_toy dataset contains 6 euclidean trajectories: Visualise raw trajectories:
code: head(traclus_toy) #>   traj_id  x y #> 1     TR1  0 0 #> 2     TR1 10 2 #> 3     TR1 20 4 #> 4     TR1 30 4 #> 5     TR1 40 4 #> 6     TR1 50 8  trj <- tc_trajectories(traclus_toy,   traj_id = "traj_id",   x = "x", y = "y", coord_type = "euclidean" ) #> Loaded 6 trajectories (40 points). trj #> TRACLUS Trajectories #>   Trajectories: 6 #>   Points:       40 #>   Coord type:   euclidean #>   Method:       euclidean #>   Status:       loaded (run tc_partition next) plot(trj)

title: Getting Started with TRACLUS
text: MDL-based partitioning splits trajectory characteristic points trajectory changes direction significantly:  black X markers show characteristic points selected MDL criterion. trajectory now represented sequence line segments.
code: parts <- tc_partition(trj) #> Partitioned 6 trajectories into 10 line segments. parts #> TRACLUS Partitions #>   Trajectories: 6 #>   Segments:     10 #>   Coord type:   euclidean #>   Method:       euclidean #>   Status:       partitioned (run tc_cluster next) plot(parts)

title: Getting Started with TRACLUS
text: DBSCAN groups similar line segments clusters. Two key parameters control result: eps: Maximum distance segments considered neighbours. min_lns: Minimum number neighbouring segments form cluster.  Segments belong cluster classified noise (shown dashed grey lines).
code: clust <- tc_cluster(parts, eps = 25, min_lns = 3) #> Clustering: 1 cluster(s), 1 noise segment(s). clust #> TRACLUS Clusters #>   Clusters:     1 #>   Noise segs:   1 #>   Total segs:   10 #>   eps:          25 (coordinate units) #>   min_lns:      3 #>   Coord type:   euclidean #>   Method:       euclidean #>   Status:       clustered (run tc_represent next) plot(clust)

title: Getting Started with TRACLUS
text: sweep-line algorithm creates representative trajectory cluster averaging contributing segments:  Use show_clusters = TRUE see cluster segments colour alongside representatives:
code: repr <- tc_represent(clust) #> Representatives: 1 trajectory(ies). repr #> TRACLUS Representatives #>   Clusters:     1 #>   Noise segs:   1 #>   Waypoints:    10 total (10 per representative) #>   gamma:        1 #>   min_lns:      3 #>   Coord type:   euclidean #>   Method:       euclidean #>   Status:       complete plot(repr) plot(repr, show_clusters = TRUE)

title: Getting Started with TRACLUS
text: convenience, tc_traclus() runs entire pipeline single call:
code: result <- tc_traclus(trj, eps = 25, min_lns = 3) #> Partitioned 6 trajectories into 10 line segments. #> Clustering: 1 cluster(s), 1 noise segment(s). #> Representatives: 1 trajectory(ies). result #> TRACLUS Result (all-in-one) #>   Clusters:     1 #>   Noise segs:   1 #>   Waypoints:    10 total (10 per representative) #>   eps:          25 (coordinate units) #>   min_lns:      3 #>   gamma:        1 #>   Coord type:   euclidean #>   Method:       euclidean #>   Status:       complete plot(result)

title: Getting Started with TRACLUS
text: Every object print(), summary(), plot() methods:
code: summary(result) #> TRACLUS Result - Summary #>   Input trajs:        6 #>   Partitioned into:   10 segments #>   Clusters:           1 #>   Total segments:     10 #>   Noise segments:     1 (10.0%) #>   WPs per repr:       min = 10, median = 10, max = 10 #>   eps:                25 (coordinate units) #>   min_lns:            3 #>   gamma:              1 #>   Coord type:         euclidean #>   Method:             euclidean

title: Getting Started with TRACLUS
text: key design feature TRACLUS phase returns new object without modifying input. means can re-use tc_partitions object try different eps min_lns values — need re-partition every time: makes parameter exploration fast convenient. See vignette("TRACLUS-parameter-guide") systematic guidance choosing good values.
code:
# Partition once parts <- tc_partition(trj) #> Partitioned 6 trajectories into 10 line segments.  # Try different clustering parameters on the same partitions clust_tight <- tc_cluster(parts, eps = 15, min_lns = 3) #> Clustering: 1 cluster(s), 3 noise segment(s). clust_medium <- tc_cluster(parts, eps = 25, min_lns = 3) #> Clustering: 1 cluster(s), 1 noise segment(s). clust_wide <- tc_cluster(parts, eps = 40, min_lns = 2) #> Clustering: 1 cluster(s), 1 noise segment(s).  cat(sprintf(   "eps=15: %d cluster(s), %.0f%% noise
",   clust_tight$n_clusters,   100 * clust_tight$n_noise / nrow(clust_tight$segments) )) #> eps=15: 1 cluster(s), 30% noise cat(sprintf(   "eps=25: %d cluster(s), %.0f%% noise
",   clust_medium$n_clusters,   100 * clust_medium$n_noise / nrow(clust_medium$segments) )) #> eps=25: 1 cluster(s), 10% noise cat(sprintf(   "eps=40: %d cluster(s), %.0f%% noise
",   clust_wide$n_clusters,   100 * clust_wide$n_noise / nrow(clust_wide$segments) )) #> eps=40: 1 cluster(s), 10% noise

title: Getting Started with TRACLUS
text: creating trajectories tc_trajectories(), coord_type argument tells TRACLUS measure distances: | data | coord_type | eps units | |———–|————–|————-| | Synthetic / simulation (arbitrary x, y) | "euclidean" | coordinates | | GPS tracks, ship routes, flight paths (lon, lat degrees) | "geographic" | Metres | | OpenStreetMap WGS-84 source | "geographic" | Metres | Coordinate convention geographic data: x = longitude (−180 180), y = latitude (−90 90). follows mathematical convention (x = horizontal axis). data latitude first, swap column assignments: TRACLUS warn detects x y might swapped (e.g., x values [−90, 90] y values outside range). geographic data, can also control distances computed optional method argument: method = "projected" (default): 5–10× faster haversine < 2 % error. Recommended regional datasets. method = "haversine": Exact great-circle distances. Use global datasets maximum accuracy needed. See vignette("TRACLUS-spherical-geometry") full comparison three available methods.
code: # Latitude-first data? Just swap x and y: trj <- tc_trajectories(df, traj_id = "id",                        x = "longitude", y = "latitude",                        coord_type = "geographic")

title: Getting Started with TRACLUS
text: data already sf object, can pass directly tc_trajectories() — need specify x, y, coord_type: Three requirements apply sf input: POINT geometry required. geometry types must converted first: CRS must set. CRS determines whether distances geographic euclidean. missing, assign calling tc_trajectories(): Z/M coordinates dropped. X Y used TRACLUS.
code: if (requireNamespace("sf", quietly = TRUE)) {   geo <- data.frame(     id  = rep(c("A", "B", "C"), each = 5),     lon = c(-80, -78, -76, -74, -72, -82, -79, -76, -73, -70, -60, -58, -56, -54, -52),     lat = c(25, 26, 27, 28, 29, 24, 25, 26, 27, 28, 30, 31, 32, 33, 34)   )   geo_sf <- sf::st_as_sf(geo, coords = c("lon", "lat"), crs = 4326)   trj_sf <- tc_trajectories(geo_sf, traj_id = "id")   trj_sf } #> Loaded 3 trajectories (15 points). #> TRACLUS Trajectories #>   Trajectories: 3 #>   Points:       15 #>   Coord type:   geographic #>   Method:       haversine #>   Status:       loaded (run tc_partition next) point_sf <- sf::st_cast(your_sf, "POINT") data_sf <- sf::st_set_crs(data_sf, 4326)  # WGS-84

title: Getting Started with TRACLUS
text: See vignette("TRACLUS-real-world") example real hurricane track data. See vignette("TRACLUS-parameter-guide") guidance choosing eps min_lns. See vignette("TRACLUS-spherical-geometry") details geographic (spherical) distance calculations.

title: Parameter Guide
text: TRACLUS algorithm two critical parameters: eps — neighbourhood radius DBSCAN clustering min_lns — minimum number neighbouring segments form cluster Choosing appropriate values essential meaningful results. vignette explains parameters demonstrates entropy-based estimation heuristic.

title: Parameter Guide
text: eps defines maximum weighted distance two line segments considered neighbours. weighted distance : d(Li,Lj)=w⊥⋅d⊥+w∥⋅d∥+w∠⋅d∠d(L_i, L_j) = w_\perp \cdot d_\perp + w_\parallel \cdot d_\parallel + w_\angle \cdot d_\angle three components capture perpendicular offset, parallel displacement, angular difference respectively. Euclidean data: eps coordinate units data. Geographic data: eps metres (method = "haversine" "projected").

title: Parameter Guide
text: small: Every segment noise; clusters found. large: segments merge single cluster, losing structure. Just right: Meaningful clusters emerge correspond common trajectory patterns.
code:
library(TRACLUS)  trj <- tc_trajectories(traclus_toy,   traj_id = "traj_id",   x = "x", y = "y", coord_type = "euclidean" ) #> Loaded 6 trajectories (40 points). parts <- tc_partition(trj) #> Partitioned 6 trajectories into 10 line segments.  # Too small: 0 clusters c1 <- suppressWarnings(tc_cluster(parts, eps = 1, min_lns = 3)) #> Clustering: 0 cluster(s), 10 noise segment(s). cat("eps = 1:  ", c1$n_clusters, "clusters
") #> eps = 1:   0 clusters  # Reasonable: meaningful clusters c2 <- suppressWarnings(tc_cluster(parts, eps = 25, min_lns = 3)) #> Clustering: 1 cluster(s), 1 noise segment(s). cat("eps = 25: ", c2$n_clusters, "clusters
") #> eps = 25:  1 clusters  # Too large: everything in one cluster c3 <- suppressWarnings(tc_cluster(parts, eps = 500, min_lns = 3)) #> Clustering: 1 cluster(s), 0 noise segment(s). cat("eps = 500:", c3$n_clusters, "clusters
") #> eps = 500: 1 clusters

title: Parameter Guide
text: min_lns sets minimum number neighbouring segments core segment must DBSCAN expansion. Additionally, clustering, cluster whose member segments come fewer min_lns distinct trajectories removed (cardinality check paper). small (e.g., 1): Almost every segment forms cluster. large: dense regions form clusters; segments become noise.

what: Why min_lns < 3 can produce misleading results
title: Parameter Guide
text: min_lns also controls sweep-line density threshold representative generation (Phase 3). sweep-line generates waypoint enough segments cross current position. However, consecutive segments trajectory share endpoint (characteristic point), segments momentarily counted position due entry--exit event processing. min_lns = 2, momentary peak 2 enough create waypoint — even though segments come single trajectory. Three consecutive segments one trajectory create 2+ waypoints shared endpoints, producing representative simply traces trajectory’s path instead capturing common sub-trajectory multiple sources. fix: sweep-line additionally checks least 2 distinct trajectories represented among active segments generating waypoint. eliminates degenerate case without affecting results min_lns >= 3. original TRACLUS paper (Lee, Han & Whang 2007) include check experiments use MinLns = 6–9 (Section 4.4), edge case occur. extension consistent paper’s Phase 2 cardinality check, also requires trajectory diversity. Recommendation: Use min_lns >= 3, better yet, use data-driven estimate tc_estimate_params() typically suggests values 5 higher.

title: Parameter Guide
text: tc_estimate_params() provides data-driven starting point using entropy heuristic Section 4.4 TRACLUS paper: Compute pairwise distances sample segments. candidate eps, count many neighbours segment . Compute Shannon entropy neighbourhood-size distribution. Select eps minimises entropy (sharpest clustering structure). Set min_lns = ceiling(mean neighbourhood size optimal eps) + 1.  plot shows entropy across tested eps values. Inspect visually — pronounced drop elbow curve often reliable guide minimum. Use estimates starting point, refine based domain knowledge.
code: set.seed(42) est <- tc_estimate_params(parts) #> Estimated parameters: eps = 64.31, min_lns = 3 (grid: 8.234 to 78.69, 50 points). est #> TRACLUS Parameter Estimate #>   Optimal eps:  64.31 (coordinate units) #>   Est. min_lns: 3 #>   Grid range:   8.234 to 78.69 (50 points) plot(est)

title: Parameter Guide
code: result <- suppressWarnings(   tc_traclus(trj, eps = est$eps, min_lns = est$min_lns) ) #> Partitioned 6 trajectories into 10 line segments. #> Clustering: 1 cluster(s), 0 noise segment(s). #> Representatives: 1 trajectory(ies). result #> TRACLUS Result (all-in-one) #>   Clusters:     1 #>   Noise segs:   0 #>   Waypoints:    11 total (11 per representative) #>   eps:          64.31 (coordinate units) #>   min_lns:      3 #>   gamma:        1 #>   Coord type:   euclidean #>   Method:       euclidean #>   Status:       complete plot(result)

title: Parameter Guide
text: three distance components can weighted differently using w_perp, w_par, w_angle:
code: # Emphasise angular similarity c_angle <- suppressWarnings(   tc_cluster(parts,     eps = 25, min_lns = 3,     w_perp = 0.5, w_par = 0.5, w_angle = 2   ) ) #> Clustering: 1 cluster(s), 1 noise segment(s). cat("Angle-heavy: ", c_angle$n_clusters, "clusters\n") #> Angle-heavy:  1 clusters  # Emphasise perpendicular distance c_perp <- suppressWarnings(   tc_cluster(parts,     eps = 25, min_lns = 3,     w_perp = 2, w_par = 0.5, w_angle = 0.5   ) ) #> Clustering: 1 cluster(s), 1 noise segment(s). cat("Perp-heavy:  ", c_perp$n_clusters, "clusters\n") #> Perp-heavy:   1 clusters

title: Parameter Guide
text: gamma parameter tc_represent() (tc_traclus()) controls minimum spacing waypoints representative trajectory. default value 1 works well cases: Smaller gamma: waypoints, higher fidelity underlying segments. Larger gamma: Fewer waypoints, smoother representative.
code: repr1 <- tc_represent(c2, gamma = 1) #> Representatives: 1 trajectory(ies). repr5 <- tc_represent(c2, gamma = 5) #> Representatives: 1 trajectory(ies).  cat("gamma = 1:", nrow(repr1$representatives), "total waypoints\n") #> gamma = 1: 10 total waypoints cat("gamma = 5:", nrow(repr5$representatives), "total waypoints\n") #> gamma = 5: 6 total waypoints

title: Parameter Guide
text: Practical workflow: Run tc_estimate_params() data-driven starting point. Try suggested values tc_traclus(). Inspect plot() summary() output. Adjust eps (fewer clusters) (clusters) needed. Fine-tune min_lns noise/cluster balance .

title: Real-World Example: Hurricane Tracks
text: vignette demonstrates TRACLUS geographic trajectory data using Atlantic hurricane tracks HURDAT2 format. bundled dataset contains 826 named Atlantic storms 1950 2004 22,000 track points. internet connection required.

title: Real-World Example: Hurricane Tracks
text: tc_read_hurdat2() function parses NOAA’s HURDAT2 Best-Track format, converting West longitudes negative values filtering storms fewer minimum number track points:
code: library(TRACLUS)  filepath <- system.file("extdata", "hurdat2_1950_2004.txt",   package = "TRACLUS" ) storms <- tc_read_hurdat2(filepath, min_points = 3) #> Filtered 1 storm(s) with < 3 points. cat("Storms:", length(unique(storms$storm_id)), "\n") #> Storms: 825 cat("Points:", nrow(storms), "\n") #> Points: 22453 head(storms) #>   storm_id  lat   lon #> 1 AL011950 17.1 -55.5 #> 2 AL011950 17.7 -56.3 #> 3 AL011950 18.2 -57.4 #> 4 AL011950 19.0 -58.6 #> 5 AL011950 20.0 -60.0 #> 6 AL011950 20.7 -61.1

title: Real-World Example: Hurricane Tracks
text: full dataset contains hundreds storms. demonstration interactive exploration, practical work subset. Use min_points select storms long tracks (tend significant hurricanes):
code: # Select only storms with >= 80 track points (long-lived storms) long_storms <- tc_read_hurdat2(filepath, min_points = 80) #> Filtered 813 storm(s) with < 80 points. cat("Long-lived storms:", length(unique(long_storms$storm_id)), "\n") #> Long-lived storms: 13 cat("Points:", nrow(long_storms), "\n") #> Points: 1190

text: geographic data, distances metres. title: Real-World Example: Hurricane Tracks
text: production analyses 800+ storms, clustering step involves O(n²) pairwise distance comparisons may take several minutes. Consider: Using method = "projected" (default) reduce computation time 5–10× compared "haversine" negligible accuracy loss. Using tc_estimate_params() full partitions find good starting parameters. Pre-filtering storms year range geographic region. Starting higher min_points threshold reduce number segments.

title: Geographic Distance Methods in TRACLUS
text: TRACLUS supports two coordinate systems: Euclidean (coord_type = "euclidean"): Standard 2D Cartesian coordinates. Distances computed using Euclidean geometry. Geographic (coord_type = "geographic"): Longitude/latitude coordinates sphere. Three distance methods available via method argument. vignette explains three geographic distance methods use one.

title: Geographic Distance Methods in TRACLUS
text: coord_type = "geographic", method argument controls distances measured: Rule thumb: Use method = "projected" (default geographic data) regional datasets hurricane basins, city taxi data, vessel AIS tracks. 5–10× faster haversine less 2 % error. Use method = "haversine" dataset spans large portions globe (e.g., transoceanic flight paths) exact distances required. Use method = "euclidean" replicate original TRACLUS paper results exactly. Degree-unit distances physically meaningful.
code: library(TRACLUS)  filepath <- system.file("extdata", "hurdat2_1950_2004.txt",   package = "TRACLUS" ) storms <- tc_read_hurdat2(filepath, min_points = 80) #> Filtered 813 storm(s) with < 80 points.  # Default: method = NULL resolves to "projected" for geographic data trj_proj <- tc_trajectories(storms,   traj_id = "storm_id",   x = "lon", y = "lat",   coord_type = "geographic" ) #> Warning: Removed 3 consecutive duplicate point(s). #> Loaded 13 trajectories (1187 points).  # Explicit haversine for comparison trj_hav <- tc_trajectories(storms,   traj_id = "storm_id",   x = "lon", y = "lat",   coord_type = "geographic",   method = "haversine" ) #> Warning: Removed 3 consecutive duplicate point(s). #> Loaded 13 trajectories (1187 points).  cat("projected method:", trj_proj$method, "\n") #> projected method: haversine cat("haversine method:", trj_hav$method, "\n") #> haversine method: haversine

title: Geographic Distance Methods in TRACLUS
text: method = "projected" applies equirectangular projection convert longitude/latitude coordinates metres computing distances: x′=lon×cos(φ‾)×111,320x' = \text{lon} \times \cos(\bar{\varphi}) \times 111{,}320y′=lat×111,320y' = \text{lat} \times 111{,}320 φ‾\bar{\varphi} mean latitude trajectory points (computed tc_trajectories()). Euclidean distances projected coordinates used throughout pipeline. sweep-line generates representative waypoints projected coordinates, inverse-projected back longitude/latitude. projection accurate datasets cover limited latitudinal range (several thousand kilometres). error grows high latitudes large geographic extents, "haversine" preferred.

title: Geographic Distance Methods in TRACLUS
text: coord_type = "geographic", three distance components use spherical geometry method = "haversine", euclidean geometry projected coordinates method = "projected".

title: Geographic Distance Methods in TRACLUS
text: Uses cross-track distance: shortest distance point great-circle arc defined line segment. Based formula: dxt=R⋅arcsin(sin(d13R)⋅sin(θ13−θ12))d_{xt} = R \cdot \arcsin\left(\sin\left(\frac{d_{13}}{R}\right) \cdot \sin(\theta_{13} - \theta_{12})\right) R=6,371,000R = 6{,}371{,}000 m (Earth’s radius), d13d_{13} haversine distance segment start projection point, θ12\theta_{12}, θ13\theta_{13} initial bearings. perpendicular distance uses Lehmer mean (order 2) two cross-track distances, consistent euclidean definition.

title: Geographic Distance Methods in TRACLUS
text: Uses along-track signed distance: far along great-circle arc perpendicular foot falls. provides signed displacement needed parallel distance computation.

title: Geographic Distance Methods in TRACLUS
text: Uses initial bearing difference two segments (haversine), angle direction vectors (projected/euclidean). angle exceeds 90 degrees, segment reversed computing angular penalty.

title: Geographic Distance Methods in TRACLUS
text: exported tc_dist_*() functions (tc_dist_perpendicular(), tc_dist_parallel(), tc_dist_angle(), tc_dist_segments()) accept method = "haversine" method = "euclidean". "projected" method applied internally pipeline functions (tc_partition(), tc_cluster(), tc_represent()) exposed low-level distance API.

title: Geographic Distance Methods in TRACLUS
text: Known limitation: Trajectories cross anti-meridian (180°/−180° longitude) can produce incorrect distance calculations. haversine formula handles wrap-around correctly, coordinate arithmetic partitioning, clustering, representation assumes continuous longitude range. jump 179° −179° (2° real-world gap) appears 358° discontinuity coordinate space.

title: Geographic Distance Methods in TRACLUS
text: Shift longitudes range [0°, 360°] processing:
code: # Shift to [0, 360] before creating trajectories my_data$lon <- ifelse(my_data$lon < 0, my_data$lon + 360, my_data$lon)  trj <- tc_trajectories(my_data, traj_id = "id",                        x = "lon", y = "lat",                        coord_type = "geographic")  # Run the full pipeline in [0, 360] space result <- tc_traclus(trj, eps = 500000, min_lns = 3)  # Shift representative waypoints back to [-180, 180] for display # (tc_leaflet and most mapping tools expect [-180, 180])

title: Geographic Distance Methods in TRACLUS
text: shift works trajectory crosses prime meridian (0°) anti-meridian (180°). transpacific routes span , say, East Asia Americas, shifted coordinates may still discontinuous. robust solution require segment-level longitude unwrapping, implemented version 1. documented honestly — datasets, users consider pre-processing coordinates splitting trajectories anti-meridian.

title: Trajectory Clustering Using Line Segments
text: TRACLUS implements TRAjectory CLUStering algorithm Lee, Han & Whang (SIGMOD 2007) self-contained R package. algorithm works three phases: Partition trajectories line segments using Minimum Description Length (MDL). Cluster segments via density-based spatial clustering (DBSCAN). Represent cluster representative trajectory using sweep-line algorithm. euclidean geographic (haversine / spherical) distance calculations supported. Geographic data can visualised interactive Leaflet maps.

code: library(TRACLUS)  # Built-in toy dataset (euclidean) trj <- tc_trajectories(traclus_toy, traj_id = "traj_id",                        x = "x", y = "y", coord_type = "euclidean")  # Full pipeline in one call result <- tc_traclus(trj, eps = 25, min_lns = 3) result plot(result)  # Step-by-step workflow parts <- tc_partition(trj) clust <- tc_cluster(parts, eps = 25, min_lns = 3) repr  <- tc_represent(clust) plot(repr)

title: TRACLUS: Trajectory Clustering Using Line Segments — TRACLUS-package
text: Implements TRACLUS (TRAjectory CLUStering) algorithm partitioning, clustering, summarizing trajectory data. algorithm works three phases: (1) partition trajectories line segments using minimum description length (MDL), (2) cluster segments using density-based spatial clustering (DBSCAN), (3) generate representative trajectories using sweep-line algorithm. Supports euclidean, spherical (haversine), equirectangular-projected distance calculations.

title: Cluster line segments using density-based clustering — tc_cluster
text: x tc_partitions object created tc_partition(). eps Positive numeric distance threshold. Segments within eps distance considered neighbours. Unit: meters method = "haversine" "projected", coordinate units method = "euclidean". Required — default. Use trial error tc_estimate_params() guidance. min_lns Positive integer (>= 1). Minimum neighbourhood size segment core segment DBSCAN, minimum number distinct source trajectories cluster retained. Required — default. w_perp Non-negative weight perpendicular distance (default 1). w_par Non-negative weight parallel distance (default 1). w_angle Non-negative weight angle distance (default 1). verbose Logical; TRUE (default), prints informative summary clustering.

title: Cluster line segments using density-based clustering — tc_cluster
text: tc_clusters S3 object (list) elements: segments data.frame columns traj_id, seg_id, sx, sy, ex, ey, cluster_id (integer, NA noise). cluster_summary data.frame per-cluster statistics: cluster_id, n_segments, n_trajectories. n_clusters Integer number clusters (excluding noise). n_noise Integer number noise segments. params List eps, min_lns, w_perp, w_par, w_angle. partitions Reference input tc_partitions object. coord_type Coordinate type inherited input. method Distance method inherited input.

title: Cluster line segments using density-based clustering — tc_cluster
text: Applies modified DBSCAN algorithm group similar line segments clusters. Segments dense enough classified noise (cluster_id = NA). clustering, trajectory cardinality check removes clusters whose segments originate fewer min_lns distinct trajectories (Paper Section 4.3). weighted distance two segments : dist = w_perp * d_perp + w_par * d_par + w_angle * d_angle (Paper Section 2.3). Early termination skips remaining components accumulated distance already exceeds eps. DBSCAN modification paper: noise segments CAN absorbed cluster expansion, trigger expansion (Paper Figure 12, Lines 23-26). Choosing min_lns: paper recommends min_lns = avg|N_eps(L)| + 1 3, typically yielding values 5–9 (Section 4.4). Use tc_estimate_params() data-driven starting point. Values 3 can lead clusters dominated single trajectory degenerate representative trajectories — see tc_represent() details trajectory diversity check mitigates .

title: Cluster line segments using density-based clustering — tc_cluster
code: trj <- tc_trajectories(traclus_toy,   traj_id = "traj_id",   x = "x", y = "y", coord_type = "euclidean" ) #> Loaded 6 trajectories (40 points). parts <- tc_partition(trj) #> Partitioned 6 trajectories into 10 line segments. # \donttest{ clust <- tc_cluster(parts, eps = 10, min_lns = 3) #> Warning: No clusters found (all segments are noise). Consider increasing 'eps' or decreasing 'min_lns'. #> Clustering: 0 cluster(s), 10 noise segment(s). print(clust) #> TRACLUS Clusters #>   Clusters:     0 #>   Noise segs:   10 #>   Total segs:   10 #>   eps:          10 (coordinate units) #>   min_lns:      3 #>   Coord type:   euclidean #>   Method:       euclidean #>   Status:       clustered (run tc_represent next) # }

title: Perpendicular distance between two line segments — tc_dist_perpendicular
text: Computes perpendicular distance two line segments defined TRACLUS paper (Definition 1). longer segment treated reference line (Li), endpoints shorter segment (Lj) projected onto line (segment) Li. two perpendicular distances combined using Lehmer mean order 2: \(d_\perp = (l_1^2 + l_2^2) / (l_1 + l_2)\). swap convention original paper applied internally: longer segment becomes Li regardless argument order. exact length equality (floating-point ==), first segment () kept Li. method = "haversine", perpendicular distances computed absolute cross-track distances great circle Li, combined Lehmer formula.

text: x tc_partitions object created tc_partition(). eps_grid Optional numeric vector candidate eps values evaluate. NULL (default), intelligent grid 50 values generated random sample pairwise distances (5th 95th percentile). sample_size Integer number segments sample pairwise distance computation (default 200). Ignored eps_grid provided explicitly. w_perp, w_par, w_angle Non-negative distance weights (default 1). Used grid generation entropy computation. verbose Logical; TRUE (default), prints estimated parameters.

title: Estimate clustering parameters from data — tc_estimate_params
text: tc_estimate S3 object (list) elements: eps Numeric: estimated optimal eps value. min_lns Integer: estimated min_lns value (ceiling mean neighbourhood size optimal eps, + 1). w_perp,w_par,w_angle Numeric: weight values used (input values, estimated). entropy_df data.frame columns eps entropy full grid.

title: Estimate clustering parameters from data — tc_estimate_params
text: Provides data-driven starting point eps min_lns minimizing entropy epsilon-neighbourhood sizes across grid candidate eps values (Paper Section 4.4). optional helper — optimal values typically found trial error. algorithm works two stages: Grid generation (eps_grid = NULL): random sample sample_size segments drawn. pairwise distances within sample computed. grid spans 50 equally spaced values 5th 95th percentile distances. Entropy computation: candidate eps, neighbourhood size sampled segment counted. entropy sizes (normalized probability distribution) computed. eps minimum entropy selected. Ties broken choosing smallest eps. estimated min_lns ceiling(mean neighbourhood size optimal eps) + 1. typically yields values 5 higher, consistent paper's recommendation (Section 4.4). Values 3 rarely appropriate can produce degenerate representatives — see tc_represent() details.

title: Estimate clustering parameters from data — tc_estimate_params

title: Partition trajectories using MDL-based approximation — tc_partition
text: Applies approximate trajectory partitioning algorithm (Paper Figure 8) identify characteristic points trajectory, connects line segments. MDL (Minimum Description Length) principle balances compression (fewer segments) fidelity (preserving trajectory shape). partitioning uses perpendicular angle distance MDL cost function, described original paper (Formulas 6 7). weighting parameters w_perp, w_par, w_angle influence partitioning — affect clustering step. Consistent Paper Section 4.1.3, small additive bias applied -partition cost suppress -partitioning. produces longer segments generally improve clustering quality. Zero-length segments (numerical rounding dense GPS points) removed warning. segments remain partitioning, error thrown.

title: Partition trajectories using MDL-based approximation — tc_partition

title: Read HURDAT2 Best-Track data — tc_read_hurdat2
text: Parses NOAA National Hurricane Center's HURDAT2 (Hurricane Database 2) format returns tidy data.frame ready tc_trajectories(). HURDAT2 format uses two types lines: Header lines: Storm ID, name, number data entries. Data lines: Date, time, status, latitude (N/S suffix), longitude (E/W suffix), meteorological data. West-longitudes (suffix "W") converted negative values East-longitudes (suffix "E") remain positive, following package convention x = longitude (-180 180). bundled file hurdat2_1950_2004.txt contains 826 Atlantic storms 1950 2004 (22,455 track points). output directly compatible :
code: tc_trajectories(data, traj_id = "storm_id",                 x = "lon", y = "lat", coord_type = "geographic")

title: Generate representative trajectories for clusters — tc_represent
text: x tc_clusters object created tc_cluster(). gamma Positive numeric smoothing parameter: minimum distance consecutive waypoints (default 1). Unit: meters method = "haversine" "projected", coordinate units method = "euclidean". min_lns Positive integer NULL (default). Minimum number crossing segments required generate waypoint. NULL, inherits x$params$min_lns (clustering value). Override advanced use. verbose Logical; TRUE (default), prints informative summary.

title: Generate representative trajectories for clusters — tc_represent
text: tc_representatives S3 object (list) elements: segments data.frame — copy segment data updated cluster_id (renumbered, failed clusters degraded noise). representatives data.frame columns cluster_id (integer), point_id (integer, 1-based), rx, ry (numeric waypoint coordinates original format). clusters Reference input tc_clusters object (unmodified, pre-cleanup IDs traceability). n_clusters Integer number clusters valid representatives. n_noise Integer number noise segments (updated cleanup). params List min_lns gamma used representation. coord_type Coordinate type inherited input. method Distance method inherited input.

title: Generate representative trajectories for clusters — tc_represent
text: Computes representative trajectory cluster using sweep-line algorithm (Paper Figure 15). representative captures "average path" line segments cluster. geographic data (coord_type = "geographic"), segment coordinates locally projected meters using equirectangular approximation sweep-line runs, transformed back. ensures gamma operates meters, consistent eps. Clusters produce fewer 2 waypoints degraded noise. remaining cluster IDs renumbered (1..K) segments data.frame updated accordingly. Trajectory diversity check (deviation paper): sweep-line checks segment density (count >= min_lns, per Paper Figure 15) additionally requires least 2 distinct trajectories among active segments waypoint position. prevents degenerate representatives consecutive segments single trajectory whose shared characteristic points create artificial count peaks via entry--exit tie-breaking. check affects results min_lns < 3; higher values, segment density threshold already sufficient. See vignette("algorithm-details") detailed explanation.

title: Generate representative trajectories for clusters — tc_represent
code: trj <- tc_trajectories(traclus_toy,   traj_id = "traj_id",   x = "x", y = "y", coord_type = "euclidean" ) #> Loaded 6 trajectories (40 points). parts <- tc_partition(trj) #> Partitioned 6 trajectories into 10 line segments. # \donttest{ clust <- tc_cluster(parts, eps = 25, min_lns = 3) #> Clustering: 1 cluster(s), 1 noise segment(s). repr <- tc_represent(clust) #> Representatives: 1 trajectory(ies). print(repr) #> TRACLUS Representatives #>   Clusters:     1 #>   Noise segs:   1 #>   Waypoints:    10 total (10 per representative) #>   gamma:        1 #>   min_lns:      3 #>   Coord type:   euclidean #>   Method:       euclidean #>   Status:       complete # }

title: Run the complete TRACLUS algorithm — tc_traclus
text: x tc_trajectories object created tc_trajectories(). eps Positive numeric distance threshold clustering. Unit: meters method = "haversine" "projected", coordinate units method = "euclidean". Required — default. min_lns Positive integer (>= 1). Used clustering (minimum neighbourhood size / trajectory cardinality) representation (minimum segment density waypoints). Required — default. gamma Positive numeric smoothing parameter representative generation (default 1). Minimum distance consecutive waypoints. repr_min_lns Optional positive integer. provided, overrides min_lns representation step (power-user option). Corresponds min_lns parameter tc_represent(). NULL (default), clustering min_lns used phases. w_perp, w_par, w_angle Non-negative weights perpendicular, parallel, angle distance components (default 1). verbose Logical; TRUE (default), prints informative messages step. Passed three sub-functions.

title: Run the complete TRACLUS algorithm — tc_traclus
text: tc_traclus object (inherits tc_representatives). full reference chain preserved: result$clusters$partitions$trajectories.

title: Run the complete TRACLUS algorithm — tc_traclus
text: Convenience wrapper executes three TRACLUS steps sequence: partitioning (tc_partition()), clustering (tc_cluster()), representative trajectory generation (tc_represent()). wrapper equivalent :   iterative parameter tuning, use step--step approach instead: partition tc_partition(), re-cluster partitions object different eps/min_lns values.
code: x |> tc_partition() |>   tc_cluster(eps, min_lns, w_perp, w_par, w_angle) |>   tc_represent(gamma, min_lns)

title: Validate and prepare trajectory data — tc_trajectories

title: Validate and prepare trajectory data — tc_trajectories
text: data data.frame, tibble, sf object containing trajectory point data tidy format (one row per point). traj_id Character string naming column identifies trajectories. Values can character, integer, factor; stored internally character. x Character string naming column x-coordinates (longitude geographic data). Required data.frame input; ignored sf input (extracted geometry). y Character string naming column y-coordinates (latitude geographic data). Required data.frame input; ignored sf input (extracted geometry). coord_type Character string: "euclidean" "geographic". Required data.frame input. sf input, inferred CRS. Geographic data uses convention x = longitude (-180 180), y = latitude (-90 90). method Character string specifying distance calculation method. Default NULL resolves "haversine" geographic data "euclidean" euclidean data. Options coord_type = "geographic": "haversine" (default) Exact great-circle distances meters. accurate, slowest large datasets. "projected" Equirectangular projection meters, euclidean distances. 5-10x faster haversine < 2\ regional datasets (e.g., hurricane basins, taxi data). Recommended geographic use cases. "euclidean" Raw degree values treated cartesian coordinates. paper replication mode. verbose Logical; TRUE (default), prints informative summary validation.

title: Validate and prepare trajectory data — tc_trajectories
text: tc_trajectories S3 object (list) elements: data data.frame columns traj_id (character), x (numeric), y (numeric), ordered trajectory. coord_type Resolved coordinate type ("euclidean" "geographic"). method Resolved distance method ("euclidean", "haversine", "projected"). n_trajectories Integer count valid trajectories. n_points Integer total number points.

title: Validate and prepare trajectory data — tc_trajectories
text: Validates input data, resolves coordinate types distance methods, creates tc_trajectories object use TRACLUS pipeline. entry point TRACLUS analyses. Validation performed four stages: Base checks: Column existence, numeric finite coordinates, plausibility warnings (e.g., swapped lon/lat). Filtering (order): () Remove rows non-finite x/y NA traj_id. (b) Remove consecutive duplicate points within trajectories. (c) Remove trajectories fewer 2 points. Warnings: Report removed, truncating IDs first 5 many affected. Final check: Error fewer 2 valid trajectories remain. sf input requirements: Geometry type: sf object must contain POINT geometry. types (e.g., LINESTRING, MULTIPOINT) must cast first: sf::st_cast(data, "POINT"). CRS: coordinate reference system must set object. CRS present, assign one sf::st_set_crs(data, 4326) (WGS-84) calling tc_trajectories(). Z/M dimensions: Elevation (Z) measure (M) coordinates dropped automatically. X Y retained.

title: Validate and prepare trajectory data — tc_trajectories
code: # Euclidean example with built-in toy data trj <- tc_trajectories(traclus_toy,   traj_id = "traj_id",   x = "x", y = "y", coord_type = "euclidean" ) #> Loaded 6 trajectories (40 points). print(trj) #> TRACLUS Trajectories #>   Trajectories: 6 #>   Points:       40 #>   Coord type:   euclidean #>   Method:       euclidean #>   Status:       loaded (run tc_partition next)  # Geographic example geo <- data.frame(   id = rep(c("A", "B", "C"), each = 5),   lon = c(-80, -78, -76, -74, -72, -82, -79, -76, -73, -70, -60, -58, -56, -54, -52),   lat = c(25, 26, 27, 28, 29, 24, 25, 26, 27, 28, 30, 31, 32, 33, 34) ) trj_geo <- tc_trajectories(geo,   traj_id = "id", x = "lon", y = "lat",   coord_type = "geographic" ) #> Loaded 3 trajectories (15 points).  # sf input (coord_type inferred from CRS) # \donttest{ if (requireNamespace("sf", quietly = TRUE)) {   geo_sf <- sf::st_as_sf(geo, coords = c("lon", "lat"), crs = 4326)   trj_sf <- tc_trajectories(geo_sf, traj_id = "id") } #> Loaded 3 trajectories (15 points). # }

title: TRACLUS 1.0.0
text: First stable release. Full implementation TRACLUS algorithm (Lee, Han & Whang, SIGMOD 2007). Partitioning: MDL-based trajectory partitioning (tc_partition()). Clustering: DBSCAN density-based clustering trajectory cardinality filtering (tc_cluster()). Representation: Sweep-line representative trajectory generation (tc_represent()). Wrapper: One-call pipeline via tc_traclus(). Parameter estimation: Entropy-based heuristic eps min_lns (tc_estimate_params()). Distance methods: Three methods geographic data — "haversine" (exact spherical, slowest), "projected" (equirectangular projection metres euclidean, 5–10× faster < 2 % error regional datasets, recommended default), "euclidean" (raw degree-unit distances, paper replication ). Distance functions: Euclidean spherical (haversine) distance components exported tc_dist_perpendicular(), tc_dist_parallel(), tc_dist_angle(), tc_dist_segments(). Data import: HURDAT2 Best-Track file parser (tc_read_hurdat2()). Visualisation: Base R plot() methods workflow stages; interactive Leaflet maps via tc_leaflet() (geographic data ). Built-toy dataset (traclus_toy) bundled HURDAT2 subset (826 Atlantic storms, 1950–2004). S3 class hierarchy print(), summary(), plot() methods every stage. C++ backend via Rcpp distance computation, neighbourhood search, MDL partitioning. 4 vignettes: Getting Started, Real-World Example, Parameter Guide, Geographic Distance Methods. pkgdown site, GitHub Actions CI (R-CMD-check 5 platforms), Codecov test coverage.
